{{Short description|none}} {{Infobox Film Festival | name = 2000 Cannes Film Festival | image = CFF2000poster.jpg | caption = Official poster of the 53rd Cannes Film Festival featuring an original illustration by Italian artist [[Lorenzo Mattotti]].{{cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2000/posters.html |title=Posters 2000 |work=festival-cannes.fr |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131020021835/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2000/posters.html |archive-date=20 October 2013 |df=dmy}} | host = [[Virginie Ledoyen]] | number = 23 (In Competition) | opening = ''[[Vatel (film)|Vatel]]'' | closing = ''[[Stardom]]'' | location = [[Cannes]], France | awards = ''[[Palme d'Or]]'':
''[[Dancer in the Dark]]'' | date = {{Start date|2000|05|14|df=y}} – {{End date|2000|05|25|df=y}} | founded = 1946 | website = {{URL|festival-cannes.com/en}} | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[1999 Cannes Film Festival|1999]] | next = [[2001 Cannes Film Festival|2001]] }} The '''53rd [[Cannes Film Festival]]''' took place from 14 to 25 May 2000.{{cite web|url=http://www.cinema-francais.fr/cannes/cannes_2000.htm |title=53ème Festival International du Film - Cannes |work=cinema-francais.fr |language=fr |access-date=13 June 2017}} French filmmaker [[Luc Besson]] was the Jury President for the main competition. [[Virginie Ledoyen]] was the mistress of ceremonies.{{cite news |date=10 May 2000 |title=Film world flocks to Cannes |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/entertainment/742295.stm |access-date=25 May 2017 |work=BBC News}} Danish filmmaker [[Lars von Trier]] won the ''[[Palme d'Or]]'' for the musical-drama film ''[[Dancer in the Dark]]''.{{cite web|url=http://www.cannes-fest.com/2000/cannes2000_palm.htm |title=Cannes 2000 Palmares |work=cannes-fest.com |language=fr |access-date=13 June 2017}}{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/entertainment/758280.stm |title=Dancer's surprise win at Cannes |work=BBC News |date=21 May 2000 |access-date=25 May 2017}} The festival opened with ''[[Vatel (film)|Vatel]]'' by [[Roland Joffé]],{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/culture/2000/may/11/artsfeatures.cannesfilmfestival |title=Review Vatel ees a disastaire |work=theguardian.com |access-date=25 May 2017}}{{cite news|url=http://www.screendaily.com/joffes-vatel-to-open-cannes/401837.article |title=Joffe's Vatel to open Cannes |work=screendaily.com |access-date=25 May 2017 |quote=needs subscription}} and closed with ''[[Stardom]]'' by [[Denys Arcand]].{{cite news |url=http://www.iofilm.co.uk/feats/cannes/compcannes2000.htm |title=Cannes 2000 Competition & Special Screenings |work=iofilm.co.uk |access-date=25 May 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305012111/http://www.iofilm.co.uk/feats/cannes/compcannes2000.htm |archive-date=2016-03-05 |url-status=dead }}{{cite news|url=http://www.screendaily.com/lions-gate-seals-us-deal-on-cannes-closer-stardom/402983.article |title=Lions Gate seals US deal on Cannes closer Stardom |work=screendaily.com |access-date=25 May
